input.prompt:
Search fbsource for files that define numbered error codes or error constants, particularly patterns like E1234567 (E followed by 7 digits) or similar patterns. 

Look for:
1. Files containing error codes with patterns like E[0-9]{7} or similar
2. How error codes are registered/defined (look for registration functions, enums, constants, etc.)
3. File paths where error codes are defined
4. Any error code registry or central definition files

Use the search_files tool with appropriate patterns. Search for patterns like:
- E followed by digits
- Error code definitions
- Registrations of error codes

Return:
- File paths where error codes are defined
- Examples of how error codes are registered (code snippets showing the pattern)
- Any central registry or enumeration files
- The format/structure used for error code definitions

<system-reminder>
Instructions from: /data/users/mengwei/fbsource/fbcode/.claude/CLAUDE.md
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Overview

This is the `fbcode/` directory within the fbsource monorepo. Fbcode is where Meta's backend services and AI infrastructure code lives. It's a federation of projects/services that run on their own servers and interoperate through Thrift.

For general fbsource repository guidance (Buck, testing, linting, Sapling, Phabricator/Jellyfish), see the parent `../.claude/CLAUDE.md`.

## Languages and Build System

Fbcode primarily uses C++ and Python. From within the fbcode directory, relative Buck paths also work:

```bash
buck build //path/to:target
```

## Testing

Tests are typically located at `$(dirname F)/test(s)/FTest.ext` for file `F.ext`.

### Expect Tests

When updating expected values in expect tests, run the test target without specifying a specific test name - passing the test name won't work for updating expectations.

## Configerator Config Access

NEVER read configs directly from `/var/facebook/configerator-client/`,
`/var/facebook/configerator-mount/`, or any path under them. These directories are
FUSE mounts of the local configerator proxy — an
internal implementation detail, not a stable API. Direct reads bypass subscription
tracking, canary support, update callbacks, signature verification, and override logic.

Use the official Configerator Consumption API instead:
- C++: `ConfigeratorConfig<T>` (`//common/config:configerator_config`)
- Python: `ConfigeratorConfig` or `ConfigeratorClient` (`//configerator/distribution/api/py:client`)
- Testing: `ScopedConfigeratorFake` (C++) or `ConfigeratorFake` (Python)

The only legitimate direct-filesystem readers are bootstrapping infrastructure with
circular dependencies (e.g., StaticVerifierKeyProvider, MetalOS early boot).

## Code Style

- Be consistent with local conventions - fbcode is huge with varying styles
- Files with the `@​generated` tag should never be edited manually
- Open source directories have files with licenses at the top; the `facebook/` subdirectory within them is not open sourced
- Default copyright: `// (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates. Confidential and proprietary.`

</system-reminder>

{"title":"ErrorTool and CodedExceptions","cite_as":"[0oPb]","url":"https://www.internalfb.com/wiki/ErrorTool/","doc_type":"intern_wiki_page","content":"title: ErrorTool and CodedExceptions markdown_content: ![](https://lookaside.internmc.facebook.com/intern/wiki/_download/?title=Notice-2x.png&id=100615&old=0&handle=GJMmoADPFF1I5EoBAMh0ZRoAAAAAbj8QAAAD) **Notice!** run `phps BuildErrorTool` on your devserver after creating a new error type. It's ok to see other unrelated error codes generated because those are likely added by others. Note that you do not need to manually ship the BuildErrorTool script changes. # Summary --- [ErrorTool](https://www.internalfb.com/intern/errortool) is the frontend we can use to create, and manage the error messages which we reference in our code. **Every** user-facing error should use ErrorTool. It allows us to group errors into namespaces, and set the copy which is displayed to the user when your code throws a particular error. Think of it as a CMS (content management system) for errors. Rather than hard coding user-displayable error copy (title, description etc) into our code - we store them in ErrorTool and reference them in our Hack code using constants. This has some main advantages: 1. It reduces the number of times we replicate error copy for similar errors used throughout the code 2. Error codes are centrally registered and thus can be aggregated and tracked in stats etc. 3. Aggregating by error code lets us accurately track how often particular errors occur, regardless of the language the user is using or the parameters included in the message. 4. Content strategists can tweak the message without requiring an engineer to help them. Each throwable error is represented by a constant which looks something like `ErrorCode::PLATFORM__UPLOAD_FAILURE`. In this example, the error namespace is `PLATFORM`. In your Hack code, you just need throw an error the correct way, use this constant, and everything will work out just fine. The abstractions you're using will get back the error title, description, display it to the user, and, hopefully, handle translations. # Creating a new error code --- You can create a new error code by going to [ErrorTool](https://www.internalfb.com/intern/errortool/), clicking on the right namespace (using the same namespace for the same oncall team or creating a new namespace if you're working on a brand new product), then clicking on "Create New Error". Before creating a new error, do a quick search to ensure there's not already an existing error code from the same engineering oncall team and namespace you can reuse. Please do not reuse error from another engineering team, namespace even if the message is similar. # Error Code Oncall --- The oncall owner is an important field for each Error Code and plays a crucial role in triaging. In the past, some error codes were reused by different product teams, leading to confusion among product and customer support groups when triaging and distributing customer-reported bugs to the appropriate engineering owners. To avoid such confusion, it is recommended that developers do not reuse an Error Code from a different oncall engineering team. Instead, they should create and use Error Codes owned by their own oncall engineering team within the namespace owned by that team. This will ensure clear ownership and facilitate efficient triaging of issues. # Hack Examples --- Say you're writing some Hack code which handles an image upload - and you hit a snag. With ErrorTool, it's super easy to tell the world what's gone wrong: ``` if ($something_broke) { throw new CodedException(ErrorCode::PLATFORM__UPLOAD_FAILURE); } ``` This exception will contain the user-facing copy related to that error. This copy lives in ErrorTool. The default handling for CodedExceptions depends on where you use them. For example: * In XControllers, employees will see a full-page error message, and public users will see a generic "Something went wrong" error page. * In API-like endpoints (GraphAPI, InternGraph, GraphQL, etc), the error will be returned in JSON format. Of course, you can use a try-catch block to do whatever you want with the error. For example, you may want to special-case the handling of some particular error codes. ## Using error description with parameters Error descriptions can contain parameters. An example is `ErrorCode::JUST_FOR_TEST__FOR_UNIT_TEST_PARAMS` which has a description of "Description with {first} and {second} params." The parameters should be passed in as the second argument to `CodedException`'s constructor: ``` throw new CodedException( ErrorCode::JUST_FOR_TEST__FOR_UNIT_TEST_PARAMS, vec[1, 2] ); // Throws an error with a description of "Description with 1 and 2 params." ``` ## Getting the raw error text There are a small number of situations in which you'll need to pass the raw error copy around. One example is when an image upload fails in an iframe. and you need to pass the error summary and description back to the window.parent for display. If you really really have to do this, you can do the following: ``` if ($something_broke) { $summary = ErrorData::getSummary(ErrorCode::PLATFORM__UPLOAD_FAILURE); $description = ErrorData::getDescription(ErrorCode::PLATFORM__UPLOAD_FAILURE); $js_callback = jsprintf('window.parent.someCallback($s, $s)', $summary, $description); } ``` You can then echo the `$js_callback` within a script tag within the iframe, and the parent window's someCallback function will be passed two - already internationalized - strings which can be displayed to the user. # JavaScript Examples --- There are several functions to use error codes and messages from JavaScript: * `errorCode`, `errorSummary` and `errorDesc`. Example: ``` errorCode('ADPRO__ADGROUP_MISSING_FRIENDLY_NAME') // 1487001 errorSummary('ADPRO__ADGROUP_MISSING_FRIENDLY_NAME') // "Ad Name Missing" errorDesc('ADPRO__ADGROUP_MISSING_FRIENDLY_NAME') // "Please give your ad a name." ``` * `errorDesc.withParams` for parameterised error messages: ``` // The ad set budget you've set is too low. You bid $1.00, but a daily budget needs to be at least $5.00. errorDesc.withParams('ADPRO__ADGROUP_CAMPAIGN_BUDGET_TOO_LOW_FOR_ADGROUP_BID', { bid: '$1.00', minimum_daily_budget: '$5.00', }); ``` * `codedError`, the JavaScript equivalent of CodedException in PHP-land. Pass it an error enum name and you'll get an Error object populated with code, summary and description fields: ``` throw codedError('ADPRO__ADGROUP_MISSING_FRIENDLY_NAME'); // Uncaught Error: Ad Name Missing: Please give your ad a name. ``` All of the strings are compiled to `fbt` calls, so they are properly localised based on the user's locale. # Rendering the raw error text in React --- Sometimes you may want to render an error inline inside of your React page instead of using the existing framework-level dialogs. In that case, you can generally take the error message and display it directly: Example: ``` component SomeComponentThatTakesError( error: Error, ) { return {error.message}; } ``` However, React's escaping mechanism prevents HTML from being rendered directly onto a page in order to prevent cross-site scripting security issue. If your error contains HTML, you will need to explicitly allow the message to be rendered as HTML. To support messages both with and without HTML, you will need to check if there is an __html property on the error message (it is automatically added by our frameworks). Example: ``` component SomeComponentThatTakesError( error: Error, ) { // If this message contains xhp and it has a __html property // then we need to set it as innerHTML, otherwise it's plain text if (error.message.__html) { return ; } else { return {error.message}; } ``` ![](https://lookaside.internmc.facebook.com/intern/wiki/_download/?title=Warning-2x.png&id=171642&old=0&handle=GKYVFwDG3j0OWd8BAL8XT2MAAAAAbj8QAAAD) **Warning!** Note that you should be sure not to manually create objects with `__html` attributes in JS or PHP and pass them in here. The only objects you should be passing in are those explicitly created via XHP on the server-side. Failure to do so potentially introduces a cross-site scripting vulnerability in the site. # Searching Errortool --- Given the body text of an error message, you can try to look up the error ID or namespace by searching in the intern UI # Translating Errors --- For translation of error messages, complete a [translation request](https://fburl.com/i18n_request). Use the **UI strings** request type. To request translation of a single error message, you can use the "Translate" button in ErrorTool: ![](https://lookaside.internmc.facebook.com/intern/wiki/_download/?title=Translate.PNG&id=283189&old=0&handle=GMpKEwIN5LApSOwAAAAAAABowTIxbj8QAAAD) You can request translation for several errors at once by assigning the errors to the same fbt project name and requesting translation for the whole project. For more info and detailed instructions, see the [Translation Request Tool user guide](https://our.intern.facebook.com/intern/intl/help/407734319592656). ![](https://lookaside.internmc.facebook.com/intern/wiki/_download/?title=Notice-2x.png&id=100615&old=0&handle=GJMmoADPFF1I5EoBAMh0ZRoAAAAAbj8QAAAD) **Notice!** In the errortool, use the '''project''' drop-down menu to associate your error message with an fbts() project name to allow easier tracking and request for translation. Error messages marked "Public" are open for translation by the community but will not necessarily be translated. Error messages marked "internal-only" will never be exposed for translation. For more info about i18n and errortool, see the errortool-related conversations in the [Internationalization (i18n) Q&A group.](https://fb.facebook.com/groups/i18n.team/search/?query=errortool) # Editing Existing Errors --- If your error message has parameters in it (for example, `Hello {name}`) and the error code is being used in prod, you should **not** add new parameters or remove existing ones. All the code running in prod that throws the error will break, since the parameter count won't match any more. Instead, create a new error message. # Changelog / History --- The last 30 days of changes to errortool are logged in Scuba via [ErrorToolLogger](https://phabricator.fb.com/diffusion/E/browse/tfb/trunk/www/flib/intern/errortool/logging/ErrorToolLogger.php). Click the "[Error Tool Event Log](https://www.internalfb.com/intern/scuba/query?dataset=errortool_events&view=Samples)" header tab to view this. # Contact / Feedback / Support --- Please post in the [Errors @ Meta group](https://fb.workplace.com/groups/errortool) with any questions. creation_time: 2008-12-11T22:58:33Z update_time: 2026-01-27T22:00:16Z","images":[{"url":"https://lookaside.internmc.facebook.com/intern/wiki/_download/?title=Notice-2x.png&id=100615&old=0&handle=GJMmoADPFF1I5EoBAMh0ZRoAAAAAbj8QAAAD","alt":null},{"url":"https://lookaside.internmc.facebook.com/intern/wiki/_download/?title=Warning-2x.png&id=171642&old=0&handle=GKYVFwDG3j0OWd8BAL8XT2MAAAAAbj8QAAAD","alt":null},{"url":"https://lookaside.internmc.facebook.com/intern/wiki/_download/?title=Translate.PNG&id=283189&old=0&handle=GMpKEwIN5LApSOwAAAAAAABowTIxbj8QAAAD","alt":null}]}

part.text:
Based on my search of fbsource, here's how numbered error codes are defined and registered:

## Error Code Files Location

**Primary location:** `fbcode/errortool/` directory contains Thrift files with error code definitions:
- `fbcode/errortool/error_code_businessintegrity.thrift`
- `fbcode/errortool/error_code_payout.thrift`
- `fbcode/errortool/error_code_digitalcontentpayments.thrift`
- `fbcode/errortool/error_code_twofactorlogin.thrift`
- And 5 others...

## Error Code Structure

Error codes follow the **E[0-9]{7} pattern** (7-digit numbers, typically starting with 2-3):

**Format in Thrift files:**
```thrift
enum BusinessintegrityErrorCode {
  NONE = 0,
  BUSINESS_INTEGRITY__TWO_FACTOR_CANNOT_BE_DISABLED = 2859001,
  BUSINESS_INTEGRITY__USER_UNCERTIFICATED = 2859002,
  // ... continues with 7-digit codes
}
```

**Pattern observed:**
- **2859xxx** - Business Integrity (2859001-2859051)
- **2127xxx** - Payout (2127001-2127014+)
- Codes are grouped by namespace/product area

## How Error Codes are Registered

1. **Source of Truth:** ErrorTool database (cdb.errortool)
   - Web UI: https://www.internalfb.com/intern/errortool/
   
2. **Registration Process:**
   - Create error in ErrorTool UI (select namespace, set copy)
   - Run `phps BuildErrorTool` on devserver to regenerate Thrift files
   - Files are **@generated** - DO NOT edit manually
   
3. **Generated Files:**
   - Thrift enums in `fbcode/errortool/error_code_*.thrift`
   - Cross-language support: C++, Python, Rust, Java, Go, Hack
   
4. **Usage in Code:**
   ```hack
   throw new CodedException(ErrorCode::PLATFORM__UPLOAD_FAILURE);
   ```
   
   ```javascript
   throw codedError('ADPRO__ADGROUP_MISSING_FRIENDLY_NAME');
   errorCode('ADPRO__ADGROUP_MISSING_FRIENDLY_NAME') // returns 1487001
   ```

## Key Files

- **Definition files:** `fbcode/errortool/error_code_*.thrift` (9 files found)
- **Documentation:** https://www.internalfb.com/intern/wiki/ErrorTool/
- **Generator:** `CodemodTransformerErrorCodeThriftGenerator`
